key_insight: SIGMA improves multi-agent design by composing agents from reusable skills, not just by optimizing who talks to whom.
engineering_relevance: For AI systems in engineering workflows, this compositional approach better supports tasks that need new skill combinations and more robust agent deployment.
method: Build a skill library and predict a skill-agent incidence matrix for each task.
Compose agent embeddings from selected skills, then decode a communication topology over the constructed agents.
Use skill-specific mailboxes so messages are routed to the capabilities actually assigned to each agent.
evidence_signal: Across six reasoning and coding benchmarks with three base LLMs, SIGMA achieved the best average performance and beat the strongest non-compositional baseline (CARD) by 2.06, 2.36, and 1.75 points, depending on the model, with only a 0.96-point average drop under unseen skill libraries.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the skill library, so incomplete or poorly defined skills could limit transfer to new tasks.
paper_title: SIGMA: Skill-Incidence Graphs for Compositional Multi-Agent Design
